title: USPS Address Matching System (AMS) API — Overview

source.publication: USPS PostalPro — Address Matching System API

chunks.heading: What AMS API Is
chunks.text: AMS API is software used to develop address matching software that provides access to USPS matching logic, which allows users to verify the accuracy of ZIP+4 returns. AMS API may be integrated within a manufacturer's product and redistributed by the manufacturer, enabling vendors to embed USPS address verification capabilities into their own solutions.

chunks.heading: AMS API Product Package
chunks.text: The AMS API Developer's Kit contains: API library(s) for each specific computer platform; the interface definition file (ZIP4.H); a test utility (SAMPLE.EXE); test utility source code; sample configuration data files; and user documentation. The kit is available through Electronic Product Fulfillment (EPF).

chunks.heading: ZIP+4 Matching and Standardization
chunks.text: The AMS API exposes USPS matching capabilities to validate address accuracy and return the standardized address produced by the matching engine. The verification process confirms ZIP+4 code precision for mail preparation purposes.

chunks.heading: Supporting Address Quality Products
chunks.text: The AMS API software works in conjunction with DPV® (Delivery Point Validation), LACSLink® (Locatable Address Conversion System), SuiteLink®, and eLOT® (Enhanced Line of Travel) files. The AMS API Product is updated monthly via Electronic Product Fulfillment.

chunks.heading: National Customer Support Center
chunks.text: For more information about AMS API, or to obtain order forms, contact the USPS National Customer Support Center (NCSC) at 877-640-0724.
